prevalence.source: SBR - Sociedade Brasileira de Reumatologia / DataSUS
prevalence.trend: stable prevalence with increasing diagnosis due to improved awareness

vulnerablePopulations: Urban populations with limited access to care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ACR/EULAR 2010 criteria, RF and anti-CCP antibodies, SBR guidelines

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 45% adequately controlled due to access barriers

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: SBR/ACR criteria, RF/anti-CCP testing via SUS
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: DMARDs (methotrexate) via SUS, biologics (adalimumab, etanercept) for moderate-severe cases via judicial demand
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited biologic access (judicial process required), rheumatologist shortage, treatment delays
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SBR guidelines, methotrexate free via SUS, some biologics via high-cost medication program
